_comment: Pinned cross-language HMAC vectors for the image privacy proxy. Source of truth computed with: printf '%s' "<e>:<A>" | openssl dgst -sha256 -hmac "<K>" -binary | openssl base64 | tr '+/' '-_' | tr -d '='. Asserted byte-for-byte by BOTH proxy-worker/test/crypto.test.js (Worker, crypto.subtle) and MMailTests/ImageProxyTests.swift (CryptoKit). K is ASCII-printable so its UTF-8 bytes are unambiguous on every side. Vector 1 (A) contains a space and an '&' to exercise RFC-3986 percent-encoding (space -> %20, not '+'). Vector 2 (A2) contains a LITERAL '%XX' sequence (caf%C3%A9, q=a%2Fb) AND a multi-byte UTF-8 char, so it is the regression guard for the 'decode u exactly once' contract: after a SINGLE decode of the minted u, the recovered value must equal A2 byte-for-byte (a double-decode would corrupt the %XX and break the HMAC).
